name: Raan Masaledar - Whole Leg of Lamb in a Spicy
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure all the fat has been trimmed from the outside of the leg and most of the skin (parchment-like white) has been pulled off. Place the leg in a baking dish made of Pyrex or stainless steel.
2. In a food processor or blender, combine 2 oz almonds, onions, garlic, ginger, green chillies, and 3 tablespoons yoghurt. Blend until smooth.
3. Transfer remaining yoghurt to a bowl and beat lightly with a fork or whisk until smooth and creamy.
4. Add the paste from the processor, cumin, coriander, cayenne, salt, and garam masala to the yoghurt mixture. Mix well.
5. Spread the spice paste evenly on the underside of the leg (the side that originally had less fat).
6. Using a small, sharp, pointed knife, make deep slashes in the meat and push the spice paste into the slashes.
7. Turn the leg over so its outer side (the side that was once covered with fat) is on top. Spread a thick layer of paste over it.
8. Make deep slashes with the knife and push the spice paste into the slashes. Pour all remaining spice paste over and around the meat.
9. Cover with plastic cling film and refrigerate for 24 hours.
10. Remove the baking dish from the refrigerator and let the meat come to room temperature. Remove cling film and heat oil in a small frying pan over medium flame.
11. Add cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, and peppercorns when they swell (a few seconds). Pour hot oil and spices over the lamb leg.
12. Preheat oven gas mark 6 or 400°F. Cover the baking dish tightly with its own lid or a large piece of aluminum foil.
13. Bake, covered, for 1 hour 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake uncovered for 45 minutes, basting 3-4 times with sauce during this period.
14. Scatter sultanas and slivered almonds over the top of the leg and bake for an additional 5-6 minutes.
15. Let the dish sit in a warm place for 15 minutes before removing the leg from the pan and setting it on a platter.
16. Spoon off all fat from the sauce, use a slotted spoon to remove whole spices, discard them, and pour the sauce around the leg.
17. Serve the sauce separately in a gravy boat; it is delicious!
